summary: Scorching second set with standout Scarlet>Fire and fiery drums
blurb: The show delivers a hot second set highlighted by the classic Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue and powerful drum work. Jerry Garcia is juiced up throughout, while the first set maintains solid energy with tight playing.
review:
March 20, 1981 at London’s Rainbow Theatre captures the Dead in full fire‑breather mode. The first set rolls along with crisp renditions of "Feel Like A Stranger," a snappy "C.C. Rider" (complete with a quirky doowop riff) and a soulful "Althea," but it’s the second half that truly ignites – a blistering "Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain" that reviewers called “stellar” and “the first European show of ’81,” followed by an unrelenting drum‑driven surge that many noted was hotter than expected. Jerry is described as juiced up, giving everything to the audience, while Brent’s keys sit comfortably in the mix and the twin drums (Mickey and Billy) lock into a tight, propulsive groove.

Across all available tapes the consensus is clear: this night shines brightest on the second set, with “Scarlet>Fire” serving as the centerpiece, backed by a solid rhythm section and an energized Jerry. The best listening experience comes from the crystal‑clear soundboard capture (gd1981-03-20.sbd.gems.104938.flac24), which preserves every nuance of that scorching performance.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Multiple recordings describe the show in glowing terms – "scorching," "stellar Scarlet>Fire," "hot second set," and "juiced up Jerry." Drum work is praised as hotter than expected, and no consistent negative comments appear; only one mixed review notes low audio quality but still highlights strong songs. The overall sentiment is strongly positive across six sources.

best_recording.reason: Excellent soundboard source with crystal‑clear audio that captures the hot post‑drums energy and tight band playing, especially the standout second set.

key_highlights: Hot second set anchored by a blistering Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain
Jerry Garcia’s juiced‑up guitar work and unique C.C. Rider riff
Powerful, hotter‑than‑expected drum performance from Mickey and Billy
Solid first‑set energy with tight playing on Althea, Candyman, and Deal

band_performance.Jerry: Juiced up and giving everything, with a crisp guitar tone and a quirky doowop riff on C.C. Rider.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums hotter than expected, driving the second set with relentless energy and perfect mix balance.
band_performance.Billy: Locks in tightly with Mickey, delivering a powerful rhythmic foundation throughout the show.
